name: Supabase
one_liner: An open-source Backend-as-a-Service platform built on PostgreSQL, providing developers with a managed database, authentication, real-time subscriptions, file storage, and serverless edge functions.

ambiguities: HQ location varies across sources: Tracxn and PitchBook list Singapore; Crunchbase and other sources list San Francisco. Singapore is used here as it matches the official company blog post ('100% distributed, no offices') and most institutional data sources.

Total funding reported as $500M by Supabase/TechCrunch (Oct 2025), $543-544M by PitchBook/Tracxn (possibly including secondary transactions or different round accounting). $500M used as company-stated figure.
Series E round size reported as $100M by Supabase officially, but Tracxn reports $143M (possibly includes community/secondary component). Official $100M used.
Headcount varies: Tracxn reports 351 (Apr 2026), PitchBook reports 298, GetLatka reports 230. Tracxn's most recent figure used.
As of April 2026, Supabase is reportedly seeking a new round at a $10B valuation led by Singapore's GIC, but this has not closed per available sources.
